stem: A SamplerV2 result's BitArray `bits` came from a 5-qubit circuit that measured only qubits 0,1,2 into a 3-bit register, run for 256 shots. Which statements are TRUE? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: `bits.num_shots` equals the number of times the circuit was executed (256)
`bits.num_bits` always equals the number of qubits in the circuit

explanation.correct: `num_shots` counts executions (256). `get_bitstrings()` returns one string per shot, so its length equals `num_shots`, and `get_counts()` values sum to `num_shots`. `num_bits` is the width of the measured register (3), independent of the total qubit count (5) and of the packed byte width (`array.shape[1]` is `ceil(num_bits/8)`).

distractors.C: The array is bit-PACKED into bytes, so `array.shape[1]` is `ceil(num_bits/8)` (here 1), not `num_bits` (3).
distractors.E: `num_bits` is the number of measured classical bits in that register (3); a circuit can have more qubits (5) than measured bits.

verification.proof_script:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit, QuantumRegister, ClassicalRegister
from qiskit.primitives import StatevectorSampler

qr = QuantumRegister(5, "q"); cr = ClassicalRegister(3, "m")
qc = QuantumCircuit(qr, cr)
qc.h(0); qc.h(1); qc.h(2)
qc.measure([0, 1, 2], [0, 1, 2])
bits = StatevectorSampler(seed=4).run([qc], shots=256).result()[0].data.m
nq = qc.num_qubits
sA = (bits.num_shots == 256)
sB = (len(bits.get_bitstrings()) == bits.num_shots)
sC = (bits.array.shape[1] == bits.num_bits)
sD = (sum(bits.get_counts().values()) == bits.num_shots)
sE = (bits.num_bits == nq)
evidence = {
  "A": f"num_shots={bits.num_shots} vs 256 -> {sA}",
  "B": f"len(get_bitstrings())={len(bits.get_bitstrings())} vs num_shots={bits.num_shots} -> {sB}",
  "C": f"array.shape={bits.array.shape}, shape[1]={bits.array.shape[1]} vs num_bits={bits.num_bits} -> {sC}",
  "D": f"sum(counts.values())={sum(bits.get_counts().values())} vs num_shots={bits.num_shots} -> {sD}",
  "E": f"num_bits={bits.num_bits} vs num_qubits={nq} -> {sE}",
}
proven = [k for k, v in {"A": sA, "B": sB, "C": sC, "D": sD, "E": sE}.items() if v]
emit_verdict({"answer": proven, "evidence": evidence,
              "observed": {"num_shots": bits.num_shots, "num_bits": bits.num_bits,
                           "array_shape": list(bits.array.shape), "num_qubits": nq}})
